q: Did you feel you preferred doing activities alone or with others when you were a child?
examiner_note: Câu hỏi này khai thác khía cạnh tính cách (personality) của bạn khi còn nhỏ. Giám khảo muốn thấy bạn có thể so sánh giữa hai trạng thái: một mình (solitary) và theo nhóm (collective). Bạn có thể nói mình là một người hướng nội (introvert) thích nghiên cứu sách vở, hay một người hướng ngoại (extrovert) thích chơi đùa cùng bạn bè. Các cụm từ như 'companionship', 'a lone wolf', 'thrive in a group' sẽ rất hữu dụng.

band7.text: When I was younger, I think I definitely preferred playing with other children. I was quite social and loved having friends over to play board games or go to the park. I found it a lot more fun than playing by myself, which I sometimes found a bit boring.
band7.note: Trả lời trực tiếp, rõ ràng. Có sự so sánh giữa việc chơi một mình và chơi với bạn bè. Sử dụng từ vựng tự nhiên.

band8.text: Honestly, I was bit of a mix. While I enjoyed the companionship of my friends during recess or after school, I also cherished my solitary time. I was quite an imaginative child, and I often found that I could focus better on my drawings or reading when I was alone. So, I’d say I valued both social interaction and personal space.
band8.note: Sử dụng từ vựng tốt (companionship, cherished, solitary, imaginative, valued, social interaction). Cách dẫn đạt trôi chảy và thể hiện tính cách cân bằng.

band9.text: Looking back, I was predominantly a solitary child who derived immense satisfaction from independent pursuits. While I wasn't entirely averse to group activities, I often felt that they could be somewhat overwhelming and preferred the tranquility of my own company. Being alone allowed me to fully immerse myself in my own creative worlds without any external distractions. However, as I matured, I began to appreciate the unique dynamics and collaborative energy that only a group can provide.
band9.note: Sử dụng vốn từ vựng cực kỳ phong phú và chính xác (predominantly, derived, independent pursuits, averse, tranquil, immerse, matured, collaborative energy). Cách lập luận rất sâu sắc và trưởng thành.

v.meaning: tình bạn/sự có bạn đồng hành
v.example: Pets can provide great companionship for elderly people.
He leads a very solitary life in the mountains.
As an introvert, she prefers small gatherings to large parties.
He’s a natural extrovert who loves being the center of attention.

v.meaning: phát triển mạnh/tỏa sáng trong một nhóm
v.example: Some children are shy alone but really thrive in a group setting.

v.meaning: có nguồn gốc từ/thu được từ
v.example: She derived a lot of pleasure from her gardening hobby.

v.meaning: những đam mê/hoạt động độc lập
v.example: Reading and painting are excellent independent pursuits.
I'm not averse to hard work if the reward is good.
I love the tranquility of the forest in the morning.
You need to immerse yourself in the language to learn it quickly.

v.meaning: năng lượng cộng tác/làm việc nhóm
v.example: The project was a success thanks to the team's collaborative energy.
Preschool is important for a child's early social interaction.

v.meaning: người thích làm việc một mình/con sói cô độc
v.example: In school, he was always a bit of a lone wolf.
Teens often make bad choices due to peer pressure.

v.meaning: động lực học của nhóm (cách nhóm vận hành)
v.example: Understanding group dynamics is vital for any manager.
Everyone has a basic need for a sense of belonging.
The project failed due to a lack of cooperation among members.

v.meaning: sự tự ngẫm/tự soi xét (bản thân)
v.example: Spun alone, he spent much time in self-reflection.
The noise of the city can be overwhelming for some people.

v.meaning: kỹ năng giao tiếp xã hội
v.example: Playing team sports is a great way to develop social skills.
I need my personal space after a long day at work.
Teamwork makes the dream work.
The house was isolated in the middle of a large field.

v.meaning: thích giao du/thích ở đám đông
v.example: She is a very gregarious person who makes friends easily.
The old writer lived a reclusive life away from the city.
The community showed great solidarity during the crisis.
At first, his shyness prevented him from making friends.
Practice is the key to building confidence in any skill.

v.meaning: tự lực/tự dựa vào bản thân
v.example: Her difficult childhood made her very self-reliant.
He was ostracized by his colleagues for reporting the fraud.
It took some time for the new student to start fitting in.
Children's behavior is heavily influenced by their peer group.
The shared experience helped the team members bond together.
The synergy between the two companies led to great innovations.
Weekends are for relaxing and socializing with friends.

v.meaning: bị cảm thấy xa cách/không thuộc về
v.example: The new regulations made many employees feel alienated.
Children are often very uninhibited in their creative play.

v.meaning: tự giác (theo nghĩa hay e thẹn/ngại ngùng)
v.example: He felt very self-conscious about his accent.
Salespeople usually have very outgoing personalities.
She was an introverted child who found Large groups exhausting.
He was a quiet, reserved man who rarely spoke about his feelings.
The neighbor’s kids were her favorite playmates.
The school aims to create an inclusive environment for all students.
The network provides mutual support for people starting their own business.

v.meaning: mối quan hệ tốt/sự đồng cảm
v.example: The teacher had an excellent rapport with her students.
Being in a group helps children develop empathy for others.
A cohesive team is more likely to succeed in difficult tasks.

v.meaning: những nhóm nhỏ/bè phái (tính tiêu cực)
v.example: The school was full of exclusive cliques that were hard to enter.
He was at ease when playing music with his friends.

v.meaning: ở trong môi trường sở trường của mình
v.example: She’s really in her element when she’s performing on stage.
